heading: Exclusion of payments from State eugenics compensation programs from consideration in determining eligibility for, or the amount of, Federal public benefits
label: 18501 Exclusion of payments from State eugenics compensation programs from consideration in determining eligibility for, or the amount of, Federal public benefits
notes: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–241, § 1130 Stat. 976Oct. 7, 2016“This Act [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Treatment of Certain Payments in Eugenics Compensation Act’.”
amendments: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–241, § 1130 Stat. 976Oct. 7, 2016“This Act [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Treatment of Certain Payments in Eugenics Compensation Act’.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, payments made under a State eugenics compensation program shall not be considered as income or resources in determining eligibility for, or the amount of, any Federal public benefit.

children.label: A
children.text: any grant, contract, loan, professional license, or commercial license provided by an agency of the United States or by appropriated funds of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: any retirement, welfare, health, disability, public or assisted housing, postsecondary education, food assistance, unemployment benefit, or any other similar benefit for which payments or assistance are provided to an individual, household, or family eligibility unit by an agency of the United States or by appropriated funds of the United States.

children.heading: State eugenics compensation program
children.label: 2 State eugenics compensation program
children.text: The term “State eugenics compensation program” means a program established by State law that is intended to compensate individuals who were sterilized under the authority of the State.
